sglang.title: [Fix] Fix clean_up_tokenization_spaces in tokenizer
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
For some HumanEval questions, we found some spaces will be dropped in the answer. This is due to some issues in the llama tokenizer. This PR fixes it by setting clean_up_tokenization_spaces=False.
This is a reproducible example for llama-3.1-70B-Instruct.
import openai
client = openai.Client(base_ur

sglang.title: Add support for tie_word_embeddings when loading weights + support for SmolLM
sglang.author: TianyiQ
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When trying to add support for SmolLM, I noticed that its config dict has "tie_word_embeddings": true which isn't supported by SGLang, and as a result, lm_head.weight is not loaded. This problem likely exists for some other models too (not just SmolLM).
Modifications
Within LlamaForCausal

sglang.title: Revert "kernel: use tensor cores for flashinfer gqa kernels"
sglang.author: Ying1123
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Reverts #1403
This commit creates significant accuracy degradation, detected by llama3.1-70b-instruct on humaneval.
# reproduce
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct --tp 4 --enable-p2p-check
python3 run_eval.py --host 127.0.0.1 --port 30000 --model meta-llam

sglang.title: Release v0.3.2
sglang.author: Ying1123
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Fix major accuracy bugs introduced in v0.3.1

vllm.title: [Core][Model] Support loading weights by ID within models
vllm.author: petersalas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This extends DefaultModelLoader to allow models to load additional weights by model ID. UltravoxModel in particular:

Currently loads the Whisper encoder weights from_pretrained
Has no way to load stock Llama model weights into the vLLM implementation

Using a secondary_weights attribute, models can

vllm.title: [Model] Expose Phi3v num_crops as a mm_processor_kwarg
vllm.author: alex-jw-brooks
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #7861. This PR should be merged after #8657; it exposes num_crops as a processor_kwarg for phi3v models (see last commit) and adds a bunch of tests to ensure it's properly handled everywhere  in:

The max token count
The dummy data
The input processor
The default input mapper (which wraps the de

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix potentially unsafe custom allreduce synchronization
vllm.author: hanzhi713
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #8410 #8404
Use incrementing counter as discussed, but two sets of counter is needed. Also, we avoid incrementing peer counters to avoid round trip latency. Instead, we increment self counter and write to peer counter which only involves single trip latency. Using one set may cause hang (hang ca

vllm.title: [Kernel] Split Marlin MoE kernels into multiple files
vllm.author: ElizaWszola
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Split Marlin MoE kernels into multiple files to enable faster parallel compilation. This will become meaningful if new templates are added to the kernel.
This reduced the compilation time of the old (pre-#8573) code combined with an extra boolean template for zero point support (will come in a separ

vllm.title: [Frontend] Batch inference for llm.chat() API
vllm.author: aandyw
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently llm.chat() API only supports one conversation per inference. This PR resolves this.
Changes:

llm.chat() should also accept a list of conversations.
When llm.chat() is invoked, the list of conversations will be parsed into list of prompts and loaded into their corresponding format that llm

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix torch dynamo fixes caused by `replace_parameters`
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix torch.compile issues introduced by 86e9c8d caught by @bnellnm (and who figured to the fix)
Co-authored-by: Bill Nell bnell@neuralmagic.com

vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
manually tested, this will not result in DoS. a single beam search request will get "bad request", while the rest requests are processed as normal.

$ vllm serve meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
client side:

client = OpenAI(

vllm.title: [Core][Bugfix] Support prompt_logprobs returned with speculative decoding
vllm.author: tjohnson31415
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In the current code, requesting prompt_logprobs while using speculative decoding can results in crashes. With an MLPSpeculator, an AssertionError is triggered (#7742). With an LLM-based draft model, the behavior has some randomness due to uninitialized data and can "work" (but first speculated token

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Use heartbeats instead of health checks
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR moves the MQLLMEngine's health checker to a separate thread and implements a heartbeat loop instead of health checks. The heartbeat thread also checks a timestamp that the main engine loop writes to ensure it it still running, along with the normal health check of the engine itself.
This fix

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Kernel] Implement acquire/release polyfill for Pascal
vllm.author: sasha0552
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#8558 introduces the use of acquire/release instructions, which are not supported on architectures below sm_70. This leads to build errors when compiling for older architectures:
ptxas /tmp/tmpxft_0000060f_00000000-13_custom_all_reduce.compute_60.ptx, line 338; error   : Feature '.release' requires
